evals.name: review-existing-agent
evals.prompt: Review this repository for dangerous AI agent coding patterns before we deploy an LLM tool-calling workflow.
evals.expected_output: Runs or recommends the pattern scanner when code is available, then produces findings using the review workflow with controls for prompt boundaries, tool authorization, output validation, rollback, logging, and tests.

assertions.text: Output uses references/review-workflow.md and references/controls.md for the review shape.
Output treats scanner results as triage rather than proof of safety.

evals.name: high-impact-action-gate
evals.prompt: Add a feature where an AI agent can send customer emails and update CRM records automatically.
evals.expected_output: Classifies sending emails and CRM writes as high or medium risk, requires server-side authorization, explicit tool allowlists, preview or approval before high-impact sends, rate limits, audit logs, and rollback or recovery behavior.

assertions.text: Output routes to references/implementation-patterns.md for action registry and approval patterns.
Output does not rely on prompt instructions as the only safety control.

evals.name: negative-generic-security
evals.prompt: Review this static marketing site for ordinary CSP and image alt text issues. There is no AI feature.
evals.expected_output: Does not use the secure-ai-agent-coding skill except to say it is not the right fit; handles the request as generic web security or accessibility work.

assertions.text: Output recognizes the negative trigger when no AI, model, RAG, or agent surface exists.

evals.name: rag-poisoning-edge-case
evals.prompt: Our RAG agent indexes customer-uploaded PDFs and can call internal admin APIs. What should we threat model?
evals.expected_output: Loads the threat model and governance references, treats retrieved PDF text as untrusted data, covers RAG poisoning, provenance, source controls, tool authorization, data minimization, monitoring, and deletion or re-indexing.

assertions.text: Output uses references/threat-model.md for RAG and tool misuse scenarios.
Output includes customer-uploaded PDFs as untrusted input and the admin APIs as high-risk tools.
